summary: feat(lyric): keep unplayed desktop lyrics white

decisions.title: Mark AMLL line play state in wrapper
decisions.rationale: Desktop lyrics need future lines to remain white while played and current lines keep the configured font color. The AMLL lyricBgLine class cannot distinguish past and future lines, while the wrapper can derive played/current/future from runtime line objects and current playback time without changing vendored AMLL code.
tradeoffs: Uses AMLL runtime internals through a narrow wrapper type.
Keeps the CSS override scoped to the desktop lyric window.
assumptions: AMLL DomLyricPlayer continues exposing currentLyricLineObjects with getLine and getElement.

rejected_alternatives.reason: It cannot distinguish previously played lines from future lines and would affect the wrong text.
It expands the maintenance surface for a desktop lyric-only setting.

side_effects: Desktop lyric line elements receive data-lyric-play-state attributes while the feature is enabled.

summary: refactor(amll): integrate lyric core into source tree

decisions.title: Move AMLL runtime source into src/amll-core
decisions.rationale: The project now owns the AMLL runtime source as first-class source instead of carrying a nested vendor package with upstream demo, docs, and package metadata. This simplifies local imports, packaging, and CI validation for the desktop lyric player.
tradeoffs: Preserves AMLL runtime file contents during the move to reduce behavioral risk.
Drops upstream demo and test entry files that are not used by the app.
assumptions: Runtime resolution is controlled by webpack aliases rather than TypeScript path aliases for this integration step.

rejected_alternatives.option: Keep vendor/applemusic-like-lyrics and alias into it
rejected_alternatives.reason: It leaves the code as an external vendor island and does not satisfy the integration goal.

rejected_alternatives.option: Fully normalize AMLL TypeScript style in the same change
rejected_alternatives.reason: That would mix a mechanical source-tree integration with a large semantic cleanup.

side_effects: CI now checks that src/amll-core exists and the old vendor directory is absent before platform builds.

decisions.title: Exclude imported AMLL core from project lint and typecheck initially
decisions.rationale: The imported upstream source uses patterns that do not match the current project compiler and lint rules, including extension-suffixed imports and Vite-oriented assumptions. Excluding it keeps the existing app checks green while preserving runtime bundling through webpack.
tradeoffs: AMLL core cleanup is deferred to a later focused pass.
Ambient declarations continue to provide app-facing types for AMLL imports.

bugs.symptom: ESLint reported six warnings after the migration-related cleanup.
bugs.root_cause: Removed diagnostic code left unused imports and helper paths, and the update download stream used non-null assertions plus an empty catch callback.
bugs.fix: Removed the unused memory telemetry hooks/imports and rewrote the download stream handling to avoid non-null assertions and empty callbacks.

decisions.title: Show a loading modal for subscription updates
decisions.rationale: Network plugin installation already shows a loading state through the input modal, but subscription update has no input step. A small reusable loading modal gives immediate feedback without forcing the no-input flow through an input component.
tradeoffs: Adds a generic modal template that may be reused by future async operations.
Keeps the subscription update button handler responsible for opening and closing the modal.

decisions.title: Read latest saved subscriptions before updating
decisions.rationale: The plugin manager page can hold a stale subscription array if the subscription modal saves data after the page rendered. Reading local storage again at click time keeps the manual update path aligned with the latest saved settings.
tradeoffs: Uses both the reactive hook value and a fresh storage read as fallback.
Trims and filters empty URLs before starting the batch.

bugs.symptom: Clicking update subscription appeared to do nothing while network install showed installing feedback.
bugs.root_cause: The subscription update path performed async installs without any visible loading state.
bugs.fix: Open a loading modal before the batch starts and close it before reporting success or failure.

bugs.symptom: Automatic plugin update IPC did not reach the main process reliably.
bugs.root_cause: The preload bridge used ipcRenderer.emit, which only emits locally, and the main listener passed an unbound instance method.
bugs.fix: Switched to ipcRenderer.invoke and ipcMain.handle with an arrow callback bound to the plugin manager instance.

summary: fix(ui): expand remaining music action hit areas

decisions.title: Apply full-container hit areas to remaining circular music actions
decisions.rationale: Commit dc9e60d fixed MusicFavorite inside MusicList card rows by letting the child component fill the circular action shell. The same mismatch still existed for download buttons and other circular action shells, so the fix needs to cover the reusable download component and all remaining call sites.
tradeoffs: Adds the same optional fillContainer contract to MusicDownloaded as MusicFavorite.
Keeps icon sizes unchanged while expanding only the actual clickable element.
Wraps playlist action icons in explicit circular option shells for consistent hit targets.

decisions.title: Keep the MusicBar comment action on the shell
decisions.rationale: The comment icon already has its click handler on the circular shell, so its hit area is already correct. The remaining issue was that disabled state only dimmed the button visually.
tradeoffs: Adds a click guard for unsupported comment plugins without changing the visual layout.

bugs.symptom: Some circular favorite/download actions only responded when clicking the icon center, not the full round button.
bugs.root_cause: The surrounding shell was styled as a circle, but the click handler lived on a smaller child component.
bugs.fix: Enabled fillContainer on MusicFavorite and MusicDownloaded in MusicList, MusicBar, and playlist action rows.

summary: fix(about): simplify attribution and add QQ group

decisions.title: Simplify About page attribution links
decisions.rationale: The About page should present the maintained BakaMusic repository, the reference desktop project, and the QQ group without separate original author or Android project rows.
tradeoffs: Keeps the link rows compact and removes redundant attribution rows.
Moves the visible row labels into existing language files for locale consistency.
assumptions: The reference project is MusicFreeDesktop by maotoumao and should remain linked directly.

tests.command: node -e "for (const f of ['res/lang/zh-CN.json','res/lang/zh-TW.json','res/lang/en-US.json']) JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync(f,'utf8')); console.log('lang json ok')"

decisions.title: Portal settings listbox panels
decisions.rationale: Settings listboxes were rendered inside setting rows, so later rows or sections could cover the dropdown through local stacking contexts. Rendering the options panel into the setting container with fixed positioning matches the already-fixed color picker behavior and applies to all ListBoxSettingItem usages.
tradeoffs: Keeps HeadlessUI Listbox semantics and virtual list rendering while moving the visual panel out of the row.
Repositions on scroll and resize instead of changing every individual setting row.

bugs.symptom: Languages, audio output device, and other settings dropdowns could be covered by later setting sections and become hard to adjust.
bugs.root_cause: Listbox options were absolutely positioned inside the row with a low local z-index, while following setting cards created competing stacking contexts.
bugs.fix: Render Listbox.Options through a portal into .setting-view--container and position it with getBoundingClientRect and fixed coordinates.

decisions.title: Expose Telegram group in About page
decisions.rationale: Users should be able to join the Telegram community from the same About page contact section as the QQ group. The visible label is Music Club while the link points to the Telegram invite URL.
tradeoffs: Adds localized labels for the row and display name in all existing language files.
Keeps the URL hardcoded with the other external project/community links in the About component.

tests.command: node -e "for (const f of ['res/lang/zh-CN.json','res/lang/zh-TW.json','res/lang/en-US.json']) JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync(f,'utf8')); console.log('lang json ok')"

decisions.title: Replace decorative header logo with a simpler mark
decisions.rationale: The previous BakaMusic header logo used a colorful italic wordmark with sparkle decoration. A single-color mark with a compact music glyph and clean wordmark better matches the app's refined header UI.
tradeoffs: Keeps the logo as the existing SVG asset so no component import changes are needed.
Uses currentColor to follow themed header text color instead of fixed gradient colors.

decisions.title: Add an explicit forced search path
decisions.rationale: The search hook intentionally skips duplicate first-page requests for the same query and plugin. A refresh button needs to bypass that guard and replace the current platform/type result with a new first-page request.
tradeoffs: Adds a narrow force option to the existing search hook rather than a separate refresh hook.
Keeps pagination behavior unchanged for normal load-more calls.

decisions.title: Expose refresh as a scoped search result action
decisions.rationale: Search failures can be retried without changing the selected media type or plugin. The button only calls search with the current query, current media type, and selected plugin hash.
tradeoffs: The first committed placement is beside the platform selector; a later UI pass can move it into the music list toolbar without changing the force-search API.

bugs.symptom: Repeating the same search on the same platform could not refetch results.
bugs.root_cause: The hook returned early when the requested query and page were already present for the plugin.
bugs.fix: Added a force option that clears current result data, marks the first page pending, and bypasses the duplicate-request guard.

decisions.title: Insert new songs at the top for custom-ordered local sheets
decisions.rationale: Custom sheet order is represented by sortType None and the persisted musicList array order. Appending new refs therefore always placed newly added songs at the end, which is inconvenient when users are manually curating the current top of a playlist.
tradeoffs: Changes only the insertion order for custom/manual local sheet order.
Keeps batch add order intact by prepending the selected batch as a group.
Leaves title/artist/album/newest/oldest sorted sheets on the previous append path because their display order is determined by the configured sorter.

bugs.symptom: New songs added to a manually custom-sorted playlist always appeared at the bottom.
bugs.root_cause: addMusicToSheet always appended new music references to the stored sheet musicList array, and custom order renders that array directly.
bugs.fix: When the sheet sort type normalizes to None, write new music refs before the existing stored list instead of after it.

decisions.title: Use a flat list row instead of stacked item cards
decisions.rationale: The previous redesigned row still read as one card per item. A single list surface with flat rows, separators, rounded hover states, and a subtle active stripe better matches dense music library browsing and avoids card stacking in local music views.
tradeoffs: Keeps the existing MusicList markup and virtual list implementation.
Moves cover sizing from inline style back into CSS so row sizing and visual treatment are controlled in one place.

decisions.title: Synchronize virtual row height with actual row box sizing
decisions.rationale: The visual overlap was caused by a fixed-height row containing a content-box card with padding, making the actual rendered item taller than the virtual list estimate. The card now uses border-box sizing and the estimate is aligned to the final row height.
tradeoffs: Uses a slightly taller flat row to give cover artwork and rounded hover backgrounds enough room.
Removes external row shadows to keep hover bounds aligned with separators.

bugs.symptom: Song list and local music rows visually overlapped, with each row's lower half colliding with the next row's upper half.
bugs.root_cause: The row card used height:100% with default content-box sizing, so its padding increased actual height beyond the virtual row height. The card-like shadow also made the mismatch more visible.
bugs.fix: Set the row card to border-box, align the virtual estimate to the actual row height, remove per-row card shadow, and use separator-based flat rows.

decisions.title: Remove the legacy MusicSheetlike card variant
decisions.rationale: Playlist, album, and recommendation sheet entries now share the cover-overlay treatment, so the older card variant was redundant and kept style branches alive across MusicSheetlikeItem and MusicSheetlikeList.
tradeoffs: Keeps the reusable MusicSheetlike components focused on the current overlay layout.
Requires affected callers to use the unified presentation instead of passing a legacy card mode.

decisions.title: Refresh README showcase assets with current UI states
decisions.rationale: The visual documentation should match the current player, home, playlist, settings, theme market, toplist, and dark mode screens after the sheet presentation cleanup.
tradeoffs: Updates documentation images in the same commit as the visible UI refactor to keep README screenshots aligned.

summary: fix(lyric): prevent desktop lyric border artifacts

decisions.title: Show desktop lyric windows only after first renderer paint
decisions.rationale: The transparent lyric BrowserWindow was created without show:false, so Electron could display the native transparent window before React and CSS finished the first paint. Deferring display until ready-to-show removes the startup edge flash while preserving a fallback timer for renderer failures.
tradeoffs: New lyric windows show themselves from the ready-to-show path instead of showLyricWindow showing them immediately.
A 2s fallback prevents a hidden window if ready-to-show does not fire.

decisions.title: Disable the native Windows thick frame for desktop lyrics
decisions.rationale: The remaining persistent black line was not a CSS edge; it matched the Windows DWM non-client border exposed by a frameless transparent BrowserWindow. The lyric window must remain resizable because desktop lyric sizing is a core feature, so the fix keeps resizable:true and disables only the thick frame/shadow/rounded-corner edge rendering.
tradeoffs: Native frame/shadow rendering is disabled for the lyric overlay while window resizing remains enabled.
Custom resize handles and Electron resize constraints stay available.

decisions.title: Force a Windows transparent-window recomposition after showing
decisions.rationale: Some Windows/Electron compositor paths can retain a stale transparent edge even after CSS has settled. A one-pixel width nudge after show forces DWM to recompose the transparent surface.
tradeoffs: Resize and move persistence are gated during the nudge so the workaround does not overwrite saved lyric window bounds.
The workaround is Windows-only and runs after show to avoid firing before the compositor has a visible surface.

decisions.title: Remove root-level rounded clipping from the lyric renderer document
decisions.rationale: Applying border-radius to html/body/#root can create anti-aliased transparent edge pixels that are cached by the transparent window compositor. The visible lyric page keeps its own rounded hover background without relying on root clipping.
tradeoffs: Root document no longer clips the entire transparent window shape; rounded visual treatment remains on the lyric page content.

bugs.symptom: Desktop lyrics briefly showed a container edge on startup, and for some users the edge stayed visible; after the first fix, a persistent black thin line still remained; disabling resizable then regressed the core window-size adjustment feature.
bugs.root_cause: The first fix handled renderer first-paint and CSS/compositor residue, but the persistent line came from the Windows native non-client thick frame/shadow on a frameless transparent BrowserWindow. The follow-up incorrectly disabled resizing along with the frame.
bugs.fix: Create the lyric window hidden, show it from ready-to-show after applying transparent background and lock state, remove root rounded clipping, keep resizable:true, disable thickFrame/hasShadow/roundedCorners edge rendering, and run a guarded Windows recomposition nudge after show.
bugs.lesson: Desktop lyric resize is core behavior; native frame artifacts must be removed without disabling BrowserWindow resizing.

summary: fix(lyric): dim inactive line-timed desktop lyrics

decisions.title: Mark desktop lyric line state independently from the unplayed-color setting
decisions.rationale: Line-timed desktop lyrics need current-line contrast regardless of whether users enable the separate setting that keeps unplayed lyrics white. The desktop lyric page now always requests row play-state attributes, while the main renderer remains on the shared component default.
tradeoffs: Desktop lyrics do a small per-frame state sync for visible AMLL lines.
The existing main-player lyric component is unaffected because it does not pass markLinePlayState.

decisions.title: Carry explicit line-vs-word timing metadata through AMLL lines
decisions.rationale: A line can contain one word and still be word-timed, so word count is not a reliable way to decide whether inactive-line dimming should apply. Mapping source lyrics to AMLL lines now records whether the original source had a word timeline, and the DOM wrapper forwards that metadata to data-lyric-timing.
tradeoffs: Adds a BakaMusic-specific metadata field to mapped AMLL lines.
The metadata is a plain structured-clone-safe property so AMLL's cloning step preserves it.

decisions.title: Use separate brightness behavior for line-timed and word-timed lyrics
decisions.rationale: The lyric brightness setting feels different between word-timed and line-timed lyrics. Word-timed lyrics should continue using the configured inactive brightness, while line-timed non-current rows use a fixed 70% opacity for stable row contrast.
tradeoffs: Line-timed inactive rows no longer follow lyric.inactiveBrightness.
Uses desktop lyric CSS attribute selectors scoped to line-timed rows.

bugs.symptom: Line-timed desktop lyrics kept every visible lyric line bright, so the current line lacked visual distinction.
bugs.root_cause: Play-state marking was tied to the unplayed-color setting, and the existing style only changed future-line color. Played line-timed rows remained fully bright like the current row.
bugs.fix: Always mark desktop lyric row state, propagate line-vs-word timing metadata, dim played/future line-timed rows to fixed 70% opacity, and keep word-timed lyrics on the configured inactive brightness.
bugs.lesson: Line-timed and word-timed AMLL lyrics need separate visual rules; settings that control word-timed brightness should not gate row-state data or line-timed contrast.

decisions.title: Persist the music detail cover style as renderer-local preference
decisions.rationale: The cover style is a main-renderer UI preference only, so it uses the existing user preference EventEmitter path instead of extending AppConfig or IPC contracts.
tradeoffs: The preference is local to the renderer and does not appear in settings.
The lyric toolbar selector and MusicDetail surface stay synchronized through useUserPreference.

decisions.title: Reuse the lyric toolbar menu placeholder for the cover style selector
decisions.rationale: The requested placement was the button to the right of lyric search. That button previously opened the lyric menu, while the same lyric menu remains available from the lyric panel context menu.
tradeoffs: Lyric font/download/link actions are now discoverable through right-click rather than a visible toolbar button.
The selector opens as a compact edge-aligned glass popover near the triggering button.

decisions.title: Use golden-ratio vinyl proportions for the album-art label
decisions.rationale: The UI treats album artwork as the center label, so a 61.8% label diameter gives the artwork enough visual weight while preserving record grooves around it. The 4.6% center hole remains visible without dominating the cover.
tradeoffs: This favors UI balance over a physically realistic small LP label.
Exact visual preference should still be checked in the Electron window.

tests.command: node JSON parse check for res/lang/*.json

summary: feat(music-detail): add selectable vinyl tonearm styles

decisions.title: Offer none/classic/glass tonearm variants as renderer-local preferences
decisions.rationale: The previous build hardcoded a single tonearm SVG. Exposing none/classic/glass through musicDetailVinylTonearm reuses the existing useUserPreference path consistent with musicDetailCoverStyle, keeping the choice local to the main renderer without touching AppConfig or IPC.
tradeoffs: The preference is not surfaced in the global settings page.
Selectors in the lyric toolbar popover and the MusicDetail surface stay in sync via useUserPreference.

decisions.title: Add an outer/inner drop-point option for the tonearm reach
decisions.rationale: A single fixed needle position cannot represent both the start (outer groove) and a deeper play position. musicDetailVinylTonearmReach lets the arm point to the outer or inner groove, and the reach selector is only shown when a tonearm is active.
tradeoffs: Adds a second conditional preference row that only appears for vinyl + non-none tonearm.
Exact arm geometry per reach is tuned in SCSS/SVG and should be smoke-tested.

decisions.title: Extract tonearm rendering into dedicated components
decisions.rationale: Replaced the inline SVG block in MusicDetail with GlassVinylTonearm and ClassicVinylTonearm components selected by preference, isolating each visual style and its reach-driven geometry.
tradeoffs: Two parallel SVG implementations must be maintained.
Shared ITonearmProps keeps the reach contract consistent across variants.

tests.command: node JSON parse check for res/lang/*.json

decisions.title: Fix descender clipping at the lyric word-wrapper level
decisions.rationale: Playback-page lyric descenders were clipped because the inline-block word wrappers inherited the player's tight global line-height. Setting line-height: normal on the main lyric wrappers restores font-metric-driven height without changing the global player rhythm or mask animation behavior.
tradeoffs: Targets the DOM lyric main-line wrappers only, so translation and roman lines keep their existing line-height.
Leaves the unrelated working-tree newline noise out of the staged commit.

bugs.symptom: Letters with descenders such as y, g, p, and q were visibly clipped at the bottom in the playback-page main lyric.
bugs.root_cause: The main lyric word wrapper spans are inline-block elements inheriting the AMLL player's tight line-height, producing a word box shorter than the font's descender metrics.
bugs.fix: Apply line-height: normal only to the main lyric wrapper spans so descenders fit inside the wrapper box while keeping the rest of the AMLL layout unchanged.
bugs.lesson: When glyph clipping follows the text box rather than the viewport, inspect inherited line-height on inline-block wrappers before changing masks or overflow.

summary: fix(amll): keep interlude dots tracking layout while paused

decisions.title: Fix inside InterludeDots.update() rather than the lyric scroll logic
decisions.rationale: The dots not following the scroll while paused was caused by update() returning early when not playing, which skipped the setTransform that applies calcLayout's recalculated position. Splitting the early-return so only time accumulation is guarded keeps the transform application path alive without touching the shared lyric scroll/layout code.
tradeoffs: Scoped to the interlude dots component only; no change to LyricPlayer scroll or calcLayout behavior.
update() now always rebuilds the style string even when paused, a negligible per-frame cost during interlude waits.

bugs.symptom: On a spacer/breathing line (the three-dot interlude wait), pausing playback froze the dots at fixed screen coordinates so they no longer scrolled with the lyrics.
bugs.root_cause: InterludeDots.update() returned early when this.playing was false, so the transform recomputed by calcLayout() was never flushed to the element style.
bugs.fix: Guard only the currentTime accumulation behind the playing check; always continue to build and apply the transform so the dots track the layout while paused.
bugs.lesson: When a paused element stops following layout updates, check for early returns that short-circuit the style/transform application, not just the time/animation state.

summary: feat(music-detail): default vinyl tonearm to none

decisions.title: Flip the tonearm fallback so the default is no tonearm
decisions.rationale: The vinyl tonearm fallback previously rendered the glass arm whenever the stored preference was unset or invalid. Inverting the guard to accept only glass/classic and otherwise fall back to none makes 'no tonearm' the default while preserving any explicit user choice.
tradeoffs: Mirrored in both the render path (MusicDetail/index.tsx) and the cover-style selector (widgets/Lyric/index.tsx) so the highlighted option stays in sync.
Existing users who picked glass or classic keep their setting; only unset/none/invalid values resolve to none.

summary: fix(amll): enlarge translation lines and highlight current line

decisions.title: Enlarge translation/roman sub-lines to the golden ratio
decisions.rationale: The sub-line font-size was 0.5em (50% of the main lyric), too small to read comfortably. Raised to 0.618em (golden ratio) for clear readability while keeping the main line dominant, matching the project's existing golden-ratio vinyl proportions.
tradeoffs: Only the active dom renderer's lyric-player.module.css changed; the unused dom-slim renderer and the desktop-lyric window (already 0.8em) were left untouched.

decisions.title: Highlight the current line's translation via renderMode rather than word-progress tracking
decisions.rationale: Keying off the existing GRADIENT renderMode (active line) to lift sub-line opacity avoids any new time/animation state and reuses the existing 0.2s opacity transition for a smooth fade. Chose 'always bright while current' over a 'flash on line-complete' pulse for better readability and far less complexity.
tradeoffs: Active line translation opacity 0.618 stays below the main lyric (1.0) to preserve hierarchy; inactive lines keep 0.3.
0.618 chosen to stay consistent with the golden-ratio font-size change.

bugs.symptom: In word-by-word (dynamic) lyrics the current line's translation/roman sub-line never brightened, staying dim from start to finish even while the main line was actively highlighted.
bugs.root_cause: dom/lyric-line.ts computed subopacity with a fixed 0.3 factor for all dynamic-mode lines, so the active line's translation got the same dim 0.3 as inactive lines.
bugs.fix: Detect the active line via renderMode === GRADIENT and use 0.618 opacity for its sub-lines, keeping 0.3 for the rest.
bugs.lesson: When an element never reacts to active state, look for a state-independent constant in its style computation, not the active-detection path.

summary: chore(deps): upgrade to React 19 and Electron 43 beta
decisions: 升级 React 生态到 v19 以利用最新特性和性能改进
升级 Electron 到 v43 beta 以获取最新平台能力
引入 @liquid-dom 依赖为流体玻璃效果做准备
添加 @webgpu/types 为 WebGPU 渲染支持做类型基础
统一 forge.config.ts 缩进为 4 空格以符合项目规范
配置 dev server overlay 仅显示错误，减少开发干扰

summary: feat(native): add luna CENC proxy and private native CI

decisions.title: Keep native sources in a private repository
decisions.rationale: Native sources must stay out of the public BakaMusic repository while CI still needs platform-specific source builds. The private Zencok/baka-native repository is checked out into ignored native/ during CI and locally.
tradeoffs: Release builds now depend on the private repository and the NATIVE_REPO_DEPLOY_KEY secret.
CI fails intentionally if required native modules or outputs are missing instead of packaging stale binaries.

decisions.title: Use a read-only deploy key for private native checkout
decisions.rationale: A read-only deploy key limits CI access to Zencok/baka-native and avoids using a broad PAT for source checkout.
tradeoffs: The deploy key must remain enabled on baka-native and mirrored as a BakaMusic Actions secret.

decisions.title: Wrap Forge ignore instead of replacing it
decisions.rationale: Direct packagerConfig.ignore replacement broke the webpack and externals plugin chain. A local Forge plugin wraps the generated ignore function and adds only /native exclusion.
tradeoffs: The current behavior depends on Forge plugin resolve order, verified by npm run package.

decisions.title: Route CENC cek media through luna-proxy
decisions.rationale: Plugins can expose a 32-hex CENC content key, and the main-process plugin method registers the original media URL with luna-proxy to serve a local decrypted m4a stream with Range support.
tradeoffs: Playback depends on the luna-proxy service and ence.node being present; failures fall back to the original media source.

bugs.symptom: npm run package failed with existingIgnoreFn is not a function after setting packagerConfig.ignore as an array.
bugs.root_cause: @timfish/forge-externals-plugin assumes the webpack plugin has already installed a function-valued ignore handler.
bugs.fix: Replace direct ignore config with an inline Forge plugin instance that wraps the existing ignore function and excludes /native.
bugs.lesson: When using Forge webpack plus externals plugins, preserve plugin-generated packager config rather than replacing it directly.

bugs.symptom: Concurrent luna-proxy registrations could receive the wrong IPC response.
bugs.root_cause: The parent listener accepted any registered/error response from the child process.
bugs.fix: Add a requestId to register messages and filter child responses by that requestId.
bugs.lesson: Child-process request/response IPC needs correlation IDs once more than one request can be in flight.

summary: refactor(lyric): remove client-side Kuwo decryption, plugin handles it now

decisions.title: Remove client-side Kuwo lyric decryption entirely
decisions.rationale: The Kuwo plugin (kw.js) now implements full lyric decryption at plugin level, including lyricx word-by-word format with XOR yeelion key and buildKuwoWordLyric. Client-side isKuwoEncrypted/decryptKuwoLyric/autoDecryptLyric Kuwo branch were redundant and could cause incorrect decryption.
tradeoffs: QRC decryption remains in the client as no QQ Music plugin implements it.

decisions.title: Keep a vendored AMLL core baseline
decisions.rationale: BakaMusic depends on renderer behavior and appearance hooks that the published package API does not expose, so src/amll-core is rebased to the official 0.5.2 source rather than replaced by the npm runtime.
tradeoffs: Future upstream updates must reapply the documented BakaMusic patches.

decisions.title: Reapply local lyric presentation behavior
decisions.rationale: The 0.5.2 group and timeline architecture retains restrained blur, centered interlude support, inactive brightness, romanization ordering, sub-line emphasis, and descender fixes expected by the product.
tradeoffs: Local behavior is tracked in src/amll-core/UPSTREAM.md.

decisions.title: Reset the playback clock before resume
decisions.rationale: Re-anchoring RAF time to the latest media position prevents a long pause from being extrapolated into a transient next-line jump.

bugs.symptom: Inactive lyrics became heavily blurred after the 0.5.2 rebase.
bugs.root_cause: The upstream blur curve started at every inactive row.
bugs.fix: Restore the prior distance threshold and restrained falloff.

bugs.symptom: Line-level and word-level romanization appeared on different sides of the source lyric.
bugs.root_cause: The upstream DOM order placed fallback romanization below the main line.
bugs.fix: Restore word prepend and line child ordering so both modes render above.

bugs.symptom: Resuming after a long pause briefly selected the next lyric line.
bugs.root_cause: The RAF anchor timestamp still preceded the paused wall-clock interval.
bugs.fix: Synchronize media time and reset the RAF anchor before resuming animations.

summary: feat(ui): default music detail to vinyl cover

decisions.title: Use vinyl as the unset preference fallback
decisions.rationale: Fresh installs and users without a saved musicDetailCoverStyle now see the record presentation by default, while an explicitly saved cover preference remains respected.
tradeoffs: Users who never saved a preference will observe the new default but can switch back from the existing selector.

decisions.title: Use a 68px mini-mode cover
decisions.rationale: The 104px-tall mini window made the previous 56px artwork feel undersized, while the initial 72px adjustment was visually dominant. A 68px cover balances the available height and lyric width.
tradeoffs: The lyric column loses 13px of horizontal space compared with the prior layout, with existing ellipsis behavior handling overflow.

bugs.symptom: Album artwork looked too small after mini mode was made taller.
bugs.root_cause: The cover remained 56px inside a 104px-high window.
bugs.fix: Increase the cover and grid column to 68px, with matching gap and corner-radius adjustments.

decisions.title: Unify recent history and play counts
decisions.rationale: The new listeningStatistics model keeps track snapshots, play counts, first/last-played timestamps, recent ordering, and total plays in one persisted source of truth while migrating legacy recentlyPlayList and playCountMap data.
tradeoffs: Legacy entries without track metadata cannot be represented in rankings; migrated timestamps preserve relative order rather than historical wall-clock time.

decisions.title: Record playback only after a playable source is installed
decisions.rationale: Counting after setTrack avoids increments caused by restoring the selected track or by media-source lookup failures. Statistics rows use normal playMusic so history never replaces or mutates the active queue.
tradeoffs: A browser-level autoplay rejection after source installation may still count as a playback start.

decisions.title: Replace the virtual recent playlist with a statistics dashboard
decisions.rationale: The new page combines summary cards, recent ordering, play-count rankings, search, top-three highlights, clear confirmation, and stable normal-flow scrolling. The old route redirects to /main/statistics.
tradeoffs: The ranking currently renders up to the 2,000-entry statistics cap without virtualization.

decisions.title: Retain the clock icon in sidebar navigation
decisions.rationale: The familiar recent-history icon preserves navigation recognition while the page header uses the statistics dashboard icon.

bugs.symptom: Playing a song from Recently Played made it disappear from the visible list.
bugs.root_cause: The historical list was also passed through replace-queue playback and immediately removed/reinserted on MusicChanged, coupling presentation order to queue mutation and virtual-list updates.
bugs.fix: Use immutable statistics records and normal playMusic from the history page; replaying a track updates its recent position and count without removing the entry.

bugs.symptom: Only about three statistics rows were contained by the list surface and later rows overflowed it.
bugs.root_cause: The scroll page also used a vertical flex layout, allowing the statistics surface to shrink within the viewport.
bugs.fix: Use normal document flow with automatic list-surface height and page-level scrolling.

tests: npm run package (webpack completed; Forge directory rename hit EPERM while Electron instances held .webpack/renderer)

decisions.title: Align statistics header with download management
decisions.rationale: Replace the decorated hero with the established page-title surface and combine four independent metric cards into one compact bar using download-management borders, radii, surfaces, and button sizing.
tradeoffs: The page loses the descriptive hero subtitle in favor of lower visual noise and stronger cross-page consistency.

decisions.title: Keep total plays as the primary metric
decisions.rationale: Increase only the total-play value to 1.3rem so the primary statistic remains legible without enlarging every compact metric.
tradeoffs: Large totals use more horizontal space but remain covered by the two-column responsive layout.

decisions.title: Remove duplicate top-three cards
decisions.rationale: The ranking now uses one consistent vertical list; top-three positions remain emphasized through rank styling without repeating the same songs in a horizontal strip.
tradeoffs: The podium receives less visual emphasis, but the layout is denser and simpler.

decisions.title: Reorganize the sidebar into focused groups
decisions.rationale: A fixed 220px sidebar groups discovery, library, and playlists into a clearer scan order while retaining full-row navigation and a left-side active indicator.
tradeoffs: The fixed width gives up the previous clamp-based adaptation on wide windows.

decisions.title: Make discovery and library collapsible in both UI styles
decisions.rationale: Both flat and glass modes gain the same information-density controls, while style-specific overrides preserve flat labels and the original rounded glass disclosure buttons.
tradeoffs: Disclosure state resets to open when the sidebar remounts.

decisions.title: Move plugin management into the discovery header
decisions.rationale: A single icon button removes the visually heavy extensions footer while preserving direct access, selection state, tooltip text, and an accessible label.
tradeoffs: The plugin destination is less textually prominent than a full navigation row.

decisions.title: Restore familiar navigation wording
decisions.rationale: Use Recommended Playlists, Local Music, Download Management, My Playlists, Favorites, Listening Statistics, and Plugin Management consistently across Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, and English.

tests: SCSS compilation for all modified sidebar styles
JSON parse for en-US, zh-CN, and zh-TW

decisions.title: Measure listening time from active playback
decisions.rationale: Accumulate only continuous progress while the player is in Playing state, bound media progress by real elapsed time, and account for playback rate so pauses, buffering, and seeks do not inflate the total.
tradeoffs: Progress is committed in whole seconds, so a renderer exit can discard less than one pending second.

rejected_alternatives.option: Estimate duration from track length and play count
rejected_alternatives.reason: Playback starts do not prove that a full track was heard.

decisions.title: Version and persist the actual-duration statistic
decisions.rationale: Store totalListeningSeconds in listening-statistics schema version 2 and coalesce IndexedDB writes while playback continues.
tradeoffs: Legacy histories begin at zero actual seconds because earlier versions did not capture progress.

decisions.title: Use adaptive localized duration units
decisions.rationale: Display up to two significant units across seconds, minutes, hours, days, 30-day months, and 365-day years in the compact summary card.
tradeoffs: Month and year are fixed duration units rather than calendar boundaries.

tests: JSON parse for en-US, zh-CN, and zh-TW

decisions.title: Combine notification state with foreground window geometry
decisions.rationale: Standalone borderless video players may cover a display without entering a Windows notification state that the existing detector recognizes, so poll the foreground window bounds as a fallback.
tradeoffs: The synchronous foreground-window query adds roughly a few milliseconds to the existing one-second Windows poll.
assumptions: A fullscreen player owns the foreground top-level window while its video is visible.

rejected_alternatives.reason: It would miss new or renamed players and require ongoing maintenance.

rejected_alternatives.option: Treat every maximized window as fullscreen
rejected_alternatives.reason: It would unnecessarily demote desktop lyrics for normal work-area windows.

side_effects: Desktop lyrics restore always-on-top automatically after the foreground window leaves fullscreen.

decisions.title: Match both outer and client bounds in raw and DIP coordinates
decisions.rationale: Requiring both rectangles to match a display within a small tolerance distinguishes borderless fullscreen windows from taskbar-constrained maximized windows, while Electron DIP conversion supports mixed-scale displays.
tradeoffs: Unusual player chrome or larger invisible borders may require a future tolerance adjustment.
assumptions: Fullscreen client content covers the selected display.
side_effects: Secondary monitors with negative coordinates are supported.

decisions.title: Ship get-windows as a CommonJS runtime external
decisions.rationale: Webpack cannot safely bundle node-pre-gyp's dynamic native binding lookup, and the legacy Forge externals plugin cannot inspect get-windows because its package.json is not exported. A small Forge plugin includes the external package and its JavaScript runtime dependency closure.
tradeoffs: The dependency and lockfile are larger, and packaging must retain the runtime dependency collector.
assumptions: The Electron 43 baseline with embedded Node.js 24 can synchronously require the ESM package.

rejected_alternatives.reason: Optional development imports fail Webpack resolution and the dynamically located .node binary is not emitted.

side_effects: Windows x64 packaging now rebuilds and includes one additional native dependency.

bugs.symptom: Desktop lyrics stayed above standalone video players in fullscreen even though browser fullscreen already yielded correctly.
bugs.root_cause: The previous implementation relied only on SHQueryUserNotificationState, which does not classify every borderless fullscreen player.
bugs.fix: Detect whether the foreground window's outer and client rectangles cover a display and combine that result with the existing notification-state check.
bugs.lesson: Windows notification suitability is not a complete fullscreen signal; combine it with foreground-window geometry for generic player coverage.

tests.coverage: notification states, fullscreen bounds, multi-display tolerance, maximized-window exclusions
isolated Forge package and packaged native binding

decisions.title: Apply desktop line-timed opacity exactly once
decisions.rationale: Normalize AMLL's non-dynamic group-wrapper opacity to 1 only inside the desktop lyric view, then apply the chosen 0.62 opacity to played and future line-timed rows while keeping the current row at 1.
tradeoffs: The desktop override relies on the current AMLL wrapper class fragment and Chromium :has support.
assumptions: Electron 43's Chromium baseline supports :has and AMLL keeps the timing data attribute on the nested line element.

rejected_alternatives.option: Keep raising only the inner opacity
rejected_alternatives.reason: AMLL's outer 0.2 opacity would continue multiplying it, so visual changes remain small.
That would also alter lyric rendering outside the desktop overlay.

side_effects: The final inactive desktop line opacity is now 0.62 instead of an implicit product with AMLL's 0.2 wrapper opacity.

bugs.symptom: Line-timed desktop lyric rows remained extremely dim even after their configured inner opacity was raised to 0.85.
bugs.root_cause: The upgraded AMLL layout writes 0.2 opacity to non-dynamic group wrappers, multiplying the desktop row opacity and producing roughly 0.17 effective opacity.
bugs.fix: Override the line-timed group wrapper to opacity 1 in the desktop view and apply a single 0.62 row opacity.
bugs.lesson: Inspect inherited and ancestor opacity before tuning a descendant value because opacity compounds through the DOM tree.

tests.coverage: desktop lyric opacity constant, fallback, and AMLL wrapper normalization

decisions.title: Keep shadows on concrete playlist surfaces only
decisions.rationale: The playlist information card, action bar, and song list need independent depth while the gaps between them must expose the page background.
tradeoffs: Transparent grouping wrappers no longer contribute an additional depth layer in flat mode.
assumptions: The header card, action bar, and music list continue to own their own backgrounds and borders.

rejected_alternatives.reason: The existing spacing was correct; the parent shadows were painting through those gaps.
Tabs, dialogs, panels, lists, statistics surfaces, the header, and the player dock are intentionally unified surfaces.

side_effects: The flat playlist layout reads as three separate cards without changing document spacing.

decisions.title: Reuse the shared flat card radius
decisions.rationale: Using --cardRadius keeps the playlist action bar aligned with other flat cards and lets themes control the shape consistently.
tradeoffs: Theme overrides of --cardRadius now also affect the playlist action bar.

rejected_alternatives.reason: It remained visibly rounder than adjacent flat surfaces.

decisions.title: Guard grouping-shadow ownership with contract tests
decisions.rationale: Static assertions keep the transparent MusicSheetlikeView wrappers shadow-free and constrain the shadow to the concrete header card.
tradeoffs: The assertions are scoped to the playlist view; new layouts still need review.

rejected_alternatives.reason: The repository has no general UI screenshot test suite, while the existing theme-contract script can enforce this CSS contract cheaply.

bugs.symptom: The gaps between playlist information, the action bar, and the song list showed a pale mask that made separate containers look connected in flat mode.
bugs.root_cause: The flat override applied an external shadow to transparent MusicSheetlikeView grouping wrappers, so each wrapper shadow enclosed multiple surfaced children and their gaps.
bugs.fix: Remove radius and shadow from the transparent view/body wrappers, retain the shadow only on the concrete header card, and use the shared flat radius for the action bar.
bugs.lesson: Apply elevation to painted surfaces rather than transparent layout wrappers that span multiple sections.

tests.coverage: playlist wrapper shadows, header selector scope, and action-bar radius
10 major routes in flat and glass modes

decisions.title: Share quality and size metadata resolution
decisions.rationale: Listening statistics and the reusable MusicList must choose the same highest available quality across qualities, source metadata, and downloaded data, with identical abbreviations and file-size formatting.
tradeoffs: The pure metadata helper adds a small utility module and a dedicated test command.
assumptions: Statistics entries retain the music item metadata captured when playback starts.

rejected_alternatives.option: Duplicate MusicList metadata parsing in StatisticsView
rejected_alternatives.reason: The two lists could drift in quality priority, labels, and size formatting.

side_effects: Existing quality consumers continue to import re-exported helpers from music-quality.ts.

decisions.title: Align listening-statistics rows with common music-list presentation
decisions.rationale: Duration, quality and size, platform, numeric count badges, pause affordance, and unpadded numbering make the statistics list consistent with the general song list.
tradeoffs: Richer metadata increases the natural row height slightly.
assumptions: Missing historical duration or quality metadata should degrade to --:-- or an omitted quality badge.

rejected_alternatives.option: Keep the count at the far right as gray text
rejected_alternatives.reason: The common music-list pattern places a compact numeric badge directly after the title.

side_effects: Recent and ranking tabs now both number rows as 1, 2, 3 without leading zeroes.

decisions.title: Establish readable statistics typography and flat geometry
decisions.rationale: Use the download filter label size as the minimum auxiliary text size, add hierarchy for summary labels and relative-time values, and apply the flat list-row radius to selected and idle rows.
tradeoffs: Larger helper text consumes modestly more vertical and horizontal space.
assumptions: The responsive statistics layout can grow naturally and hides the relative-time column at narrow widths.

rejected_alternatives.option: Increase only the explicitly named strings
rejected_alternatives.reason: Other undersized helper labels would remain inconsistent.
Idle and selected flat rows would use different geometry.

side_effects: Summary labels and relative-time values use 0.82rem while auxiliary labels retain the 0.76rem baseline.

tests.coverage: quality priority, abbreviations, numeric and textual sizes, downloaded metadata, empty quality
flat row radius, metadata integration, pause icon, count badge, typography floor, time hierarchy, numbering
206 flat rows, selected and idle 4px radii, metadata badges, count placement, pause icon, layout overflow

decisions.title: Restore the established two-digit statistics numbering
decisions.rationale: Recent and ranking rows should follow the existing 01/02/03 visual numbering system used by common song lists.
tradeoffs: Rows above 99 naturally expand to three digits.
assumptions: Both statistics tabs continue to share StatisticsTrackRow.

rejected_alternatives.reason: The requested presentation retains the older padded numbering system.

side_effects: Recent and ranking tabs now render identical padded positions.

bugs.symptom: Listening-statistics rows displayed 1, 2, 3 while the retained song-list system displayed 01, 02, 03.
bugs.root_cause: StatisticsTrackRow rendered index + 1 directly after the prior presentation update.
bugs.fix: Format the shared row index with String(index + 1).padStart(2, "0") and update its contract assertion.
bugs.lesson: Keep numbering format guarded where multiple list tabs share one row component.
